text.title: vui-privacy-security Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for vui-privacy-security as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Classifier returns the original utterance text in any field.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any echo of user text in structured output creates a PII logging path; force structured outputs that reference slot names, not values.

failure-mode.description: LLM leaks sensitive slot values into "rationale" or "thought" fields of structured output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forbid free-text reasoning that quotes user data; force structured outputs that reference slot names only, validated by schema before logging.

failure-mode.description: ASR transcripts contain partial sensitive data ("my card is four nine one two...") that regex misses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Combine word-level NER with intent-aware slot deletion; regex alone cannot handle spoken partial values or transposed digits.

failure-mode.description: Observability tools (Datadog, Sentry) receive prompt+response containing sensitive data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Configure log scrubbers at the SDK layer before the log sink; agents cannot reliably self-redact in observability calls made by the framework.

failure-mode.description: Caching layer re-emits PII because the cache key is the redacted text but the cached value contains the original.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always cache on redacted outputs only; never use unredacted inputs as cache keys with redacted-output values.
